ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for canned pear in syrup drained; not a clinical entity requiring SNOMED mapping.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 80 g, equivalent to one halved canned pear half, drained of syrup before consumption.

nutrient_source.title: Canned pear, in syrup (drained) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for canned pear in syrup (drained), CR ID F04030014. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; canned pear in syrup entry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 44 (low) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for canned pear in syrup. Glycemic load per 80 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (44 × 15.26) / 100 = 6.7. Carbohydrate per serving = 19.08 g/100g × 80 g = 15.26 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High residual sugar (16.42 g/100g) despite low sodium (5 mg/100g); elevated dietary sugar intake is associated with increased blood pressure via insulin resistance and sympathetic nervous system activation.
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is low at 5 mg/100g and does not pose a direct sodium-related hypertension risk; however, the high residual sugar of 16.42 g/100g after draining warrants caution, as excess sugar intake can elevate blood pressure — limit portion to 80 g and prefer water-packed canned pear or fresh pear.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar content of 16.42 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g threshold for type 2 diabetes management; despite a low GI of 44, the high sugar load from residual syrup absorption poses a meaningful postprandial glucose risk.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Canned pear in syrup (drained) contains 16.42 g sugar per 100 g, exceeding the recommended threshold for individuals with type 2 diabetes; if consumed, restrict to a maximum 50 g portion, pair with a protein source to blunt glycaemic response, and prefer fresh pear or water-packed canned pear as lower-sugar alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (0.18 g/100g) and negligible saturated fat are favourable; however, high sugar content (16.42 g/100g) contributes to triglyceride elevation and metabolic risk relevant to cardiovascular disease.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The negligible fat and sodium profile of drained canned pear is cardiovascularly neutral, but the high residual sugar of 16.42 g/100g can raise serum triglycerides with regular consumption — limit intake and choose water-packed or fresh pear to reduce added sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 16.42 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g RA threshold; residual syrup sugar promotes advanced glycation end-product (AGE) formation and pro-inflammatory mediator activity relevant to RA joint damage.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Canned pear in syrup (drained) is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to sugar content of 16.42 g/100g exceeding the RA threshold; choose fresh pear or water-packed canned pear to access dietary fibre benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canned pear in syrup (drained) is a commercially processed fruit product consisting of pear halves or slices that have been packed in heavy sugar syrup during the canning process and subsequently drained of the bulk liquid before consumption or use. The draining step removes the free syrup but does not eliminate sugar that has been absorbed into the fruit tissue during processing, resulting in a residual sugar content of approximately 16.42 g per 100 g — substantially higher than fresh pear (approximately 9–10 g/100g). The fruit has a pale golden to cream colour, a soft and yielding texture, and a sweet flavour profile with mild pear aroma. Nutritionally, per 100 g the drained product provides 74 kcal of energy, 19.08 g carbohydrate, 0.24 g protein, 0.18 g fat, 2.7 g dietary fibre, and 5 mg sodium. The glycemic index is 44 (low category), but the high sugar content means the glycemic load per 80 g serving is approximately 6.7. Clinically, the elevated sugar content (16.42 g/100g) is a concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The low sodium content (5 mg/100g) is a positive attribute for hypertension management, but the sugar burden offsets this benefit. Canned pear in syrup is widely available globally and is used as a dessert component, snack, or ingredient in fruit salads and baked goods. Water-packed or juice-packed alternatives are recommended for individuals requiring lower sugar intake.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F04030014) at confidence 0.95. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for canned pear in syrup. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and supported by named approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific canned pear in syrup (drained) entry are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F04030014) as of 2026. saturated_fat_g is estimated at 0.01 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for canned pear in syrup (NDB 09252), consistent with the negligible total fat of 0.18 g/100g. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source — this is a plant-based fruit product that naturally contains no cholesterol. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; it is a globally generic processed fruit product, so no mandatory local-language names apply under G6 multilingual rules. FoodEx2 code A00LC is used as the closest defensible match for pears canned in syrup drained; FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 is used for canned pear. Reviewers should verify FoodEx2 and FoodOn codes against current database versions.
